mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's predictive power decays below 0.02 Rank IC within 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit availability combined with expanding housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand growth, which lifts cyclical equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 150 basis points over 60-day windows, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained macroeconomic environment, an increase in the industrial production index reflects improved capacity utilization and margin expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO momentum (correlation < 0.15), or the signal generates negative returns during confirmed supply bottlenecks.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 5-day equity returns across multiple market cycles, or the signal produces negative forward returns in > 55% of positive GDP growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy longer than anticipated, increasing the equity risk premium and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 1.5 index points in 3 months
failure_condition: Equity valuations remain stable or expand despite sustained inflation acceleration, or the signal's out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the unemployment rate and declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate structural labor market deterioration, which triggers downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict equity drawdowns during historical labor market contractions, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for Federal Reserve policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts serve as a leading indicator for consumer durable spending and construction sector profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during housing market expansions.
failure_condition: HOUST momentum shows no statistically significant relationship with 5-day equity returns, or the signal produces negative forward returns in > 60% of rising housing start periods.
